subtitle: How Vikramaditya Broke the Western Shaka Power and Raised the Boar of Udayagiri

panels.shot: majestic low-angle wide of a rocky cliff with the colossal Varaha carved into it, Chandragupta II standing small but resolute below
panels.action: Chandragupta II Vikramaditya stands in royal armor at the foot of the towering Varaha relief at Udayagiri, the stone boar looming above him like a mountain of carved divinity in the golden evening light.

dialogue.text: In the age of the Guptas, Chandragupta the Second, called Vikramaditya, broke the long Shaka rule in the west and raised stone and coin to proclaim a new order.

i18n.hi: गुप्त युग में चंद्रगुप्त द्वितीय, विक्रमादित्य, ने पश्चिम की लंबी शकों की हुकूमत तोड़ दी और पत्थर‑सिक्कों से नई व्यवस्था गढ़ी।

panels.shot: epic bird’s-eye over a painted map-like landscape
panels.action: A painterly map of western India shows Malwa’s plateau, the Gujarat coast with busy ports, and inland routes linking them to the Ganga plains, tiny caravans and ships hinted along the routes.

dialogue.text: For three centuries, Western Shaka rulers, the Kshatrapas, held Gujarat and its sea-gates, while the rising Guptas ruled the rich Ganga lands in the east.

i18n.hi: तीन सौ बरस से पश्चिमी शक क्षत्रप गुजरात और उसके समुंदर दरवाज़ों पर बैठे थे, जबकि उगते गुप्त गंगा के सोने से भरपूर मैदानों पर।

panels.shot: intimate medium shot inside a royal hall
panels.action: Young Chandragupta II, not yet crowned, stands beside an aged Samudragupta’s throne in a simple yet grand hall, listening as courtiers gesture toward a painted panel of western coastal cities.

dialogue.text: Born to Samudragupta’s conquests, Chandragupta the Second grew up hearing of ports beyond the western desert, and of Shaka satraps who minted silver as if they owned the sunrise.

i18n.hi: समुद्रगुप्त की फतहों के घर में पले चंद्रगुप्त द्वितीय ने बचपन से ही रेगिस्तान के पार बंदरगाहों और उन शक क्षत्रपों की बातें सुनीं जो चांदी ऐसे ढालते थे मानो सूरज उनके नाम हो।

panels.shot: slow push-in on a profile close-up
panels.action: Close on Chandragupta II’s face as a young man, eyes sharp and reflective, a map of western India rolled open before him on a low table lit by oil lamps.

dialogue.speaker: chandragupta_ii_vikramaditya
dialogue.text: The Ganga feeds us, but the western sea feeds the world.

i18n.hi: गंगा हमें पोसती है, पर पश्चिम का समुंदर दुनिया को।

panels.shot: royal wide shot showing marriage alliance scene
panels.action: In a ceremonial pavilion, Chandragupta II stands in wedding attire beside a Licchavi princess, garlands between them, elders blessing the union as banners of two houses flutter together.

dialogue.text: Through war and marriage, he knit his realm tighter, taking a Licchavi princess as queen and drawing central Indian clans into a net that could stretch toward Malwa and the Shaka lands.

i18n.hi: जंग और रिश्तेदारी से उसने राज को सांचा दिया, लिच्छवि राजकुमारी से ब्याह किया और मध्य भारत की जातियों को जाल की तरह जोड़ा जो मालवा और शक भूमि तक फैल सके।

panels.shot: medium-wide of a council chamber
panels.action: Chandragupta II, now older and crowned, sits in council, listening as an envoy from the west kneels, holding a worn Shaka silver coin with a ruler’s profile and legends in Greek-like script.

dialogue.text: In Malwa, traders brought Satrapa silver, stamped with foreign names, and tales of Rudrasimha, last of the Western Kshatrapas, whose line had grown thin even as their ports grew rich.

i18n.hi: मालवा में व्यापारी सत्रपों की चांदी लाते, अजनबी नामों की मुहरों के साथ, और किस्से रुद्रसिंह के, आखिरी पश्चिमी क्षत्रप के, जिसकी नस्ल पतली पर बंदरगाह मोटे हुए।

panels.shot: close-up on hand and coin
panels.action: Close on Chandragupta’s hand as he turns the Shaka coin over, the faint profile and worn legends catching the light, his other hand resting on a scroll showing caravan tolls and customs.

dialogue.speaker: chandragupta_ii_vikramaditya
dialogue.text: As long as their coin rules the west, our empire is half-born.

i18n.hi: जब तक उनकी मुहर पश्चिम पर चले, हमारा साम्राज्य अधूरा ही है।

panels.shot: wide shot of marching army across plains
panels.action: An orderly Gupta army with cavalry, war elephants, and banner-bearers marches across a dusty plain toward the west, Chandragupta II riding at the front beneath a royal umbrella.

dialogue.text: He moved not in haste but in layers, first securing Malwa’s plateau, then feeling along the old roads to the Narmada, testing the Shaka frontiers with measured steel.

i18n.hi: वह जल्दबाज़ी नहीं, पर्तों में चला; पहले मालवा का पठार साधा, फिर पुरानी राहों से नर्मदा तक टटोला, नापतौल कर शक सरहद को तलवार से परखा।

panels.shot: dramatic wide shot of opposing camps at dawn
panels.action: At dawn, two armies face each other across a shallow valley: Gupta lines on one ridge with elephants and archers, Shaka forces on the other with armored cavalry and standards of the Western Kshatrapas.

dialogue.text: The sources speak in stone and coin, not in battle songs, yet in these years, near the end of the fourth century, Gupta and Shaka banners met under the pale western sky.

i18n.hi: इतिहास यहां गीत नहीं, पत्थर और सिक्कों में बोलता है, पर चौथी सदी के इन सालों में कहीं पश्चिमी आसमान तले गुप्त और शक ध्वज आमने‑सामने आए।

panels.shot: low-angle mid-shot of Chandragupta on elephant
panels.action: Chandragupta II stands steady atop a war elephant as lines of Gupta infantry advance below, his arm outstretched, his face calm and resolute amid the dust of approaching clash.

dialogue.speaker: chandragupta_ii_vikramaditya
dialogue.text: Remember, not plunder but order must follow our footsteps.

i18n.hi: याद रखना, हमारे कदमों के बाद लूट नहीं, व्यवस्था आनी चाहिए।

panels.shot: symbolic wide shot of battlefield aftermath
panels.action: The battlefield after combat: weapons abandoned, broken standards of the Western Kshatrapas on the ground, Gupta soldiers standing weary but disciplined as the sun sinks, no direct violence shown.

dialogue.text: Somewhere in these campaigns, Rudrasimha’s power failed. The Shaka line, once rooted in foreign conquest, ended, and western satrapal mints fell silent after nearly three hundred years.

i18n.hi: इन्हीं मुहिमों के बीच कहीं रुद्रसिंह की ताकत ढह गई; पराई फतह से उगी शक वंशरेखा टूट गई और करीब तीन सौ साल बाद पश्चिमी क्षत्रपों की टकसालें थम गईं।

panels.shot: wide shot of coastal city and harbor
panels.action: A bustling Gujarat port city under new Gupta banners shows warehouses, markets, and a harbor full of ships, Chandragupta II entering through a gate as townsfolk watch curiously.

dialogue.text: With Gujarat and Saurashtra absorbed, the western sea opened to the Guptas. Ports that once counted satraps as overlords now weighed their goods under Vikramaditya’s officers.

i18n.hi: गुजरात‑सौराष्ट्र जुड़ते ही पश्चिमी समुंदर गुप्तों के लिए खुल गया; जो बंदरगाह कल क्षत्रपों को मालिक मानते थे, आज माल विक्रमादित्य के अफसरों के तराजू पर तोलने लगे।

panels.shot: medium shot of Chandragupta at a mint
panels.action: Inside a mint, artisans work at anvils and furnaces as Chandragupta II examines freshly struck silver coins on a tray, their designs echoing but surpassing the old Shaka style.

dialogue.text: Soon, a new silver shone. Gupta rupakas, modeled on the old Satrapa coins, carried his authority into markets that had long moved to the rhythm of Shaka metal.

i18n.hi: जल्दी ही नई चांदी चमकी; पुराने सत्रप सिक्कों से ढले गुप्त रूपक बाज़ारों में छा गए, जहां सदियों से शक धातु की ठनक पर सौदे चलते थे।

panels.shot: extreme close-up of coin face
panels.action: Extreme close-up of a newly minted Gupta silver coin resting on Chandragupta’s palm, its crisp profile and symbols sharp, a faint stack of worn Shaka coins blurred in the background.

dialogue.speaker: chandragupta_ii_vikramaditya
dialogue.text: Let every market feel that the age has changed.

i18n.hi: हर मंडी को महसूस हो कि युग बदल चुका है।

panels.shot: wide shot of monsoon-fed fields and caravans
panels.action: A fertile landscape shows green fields, temples, and caravans moving along orderly roads, with distant ships visible on the horizon, suggesting the prosperity of the Gupta golden age.

dialogue.text: Years later, the monk Faxian crossed this empire and wrote of mild punishments, full granaries, and safe roads. The westward ports he saw fed this abundance with spice, cloth, and coin.

i18n.hi: कई बरस बाद भिक्षु फाशियान ने इस साम्राज्य को पार किया और लिख गया–हल्की सजाएं, भरे कोठार, सुरक्षित राहें; पश्चिमी बंदरगाह मसाले, कपड़ा और सिक्कों से इस समृद्धि को भरते थे।

panels.shot: medium shot in a quiet shrine-like chamber
panels.action: Chandragupta II sits with priests and architects beside a rough rock outcrop, plans and sketches laid out, as they gesture toward the rock face, imagining a grand relief upon it.

dialogue.text: Yet he sought more than revenue. At Udayagiri near Vidisha, where shrines already clung to the rock, he turned to stone to tell another story: of king, god, and earth.

i18n.hi: पर उसे खालिस कर नहीं, और भी मंज़िलें चाहिए थीं; विदिशा के पास उदयगिरि पर, जहां पहले से देवालय चट्टान पकड़कर खड़े थे, उसने पत्थर में राजा‑देव‑धरती की एक और कथा गढ़ी।

panels.shot: wide shot of Udayagiri cliffside with scaffolding
panels.action: The cliffs of Udayagiri rise under a clear sky, bamboo scaffolds hugging the rock as artisans carve the massive Varaha figure, Chandragupta II watching from below with a small entourage.

dialogue.text: There, artisans hewed the mountain itself. From living rock emerged Varaha, the boar form of Vishnu, hauling the earth goddess above the flood, tusks and muscles frozen in a timeless surge.

i18n.hi: यहीं शिल्पियों ने पहाड़ को ही काट डाला; जीवित चट्टान से वराह निकला, विष्णु का वराह रूप, जो धरती देवी को बाढ़ के ऊपर उठाए हुए है, दांत‑मांसपेशियां अनंत वेग में जमी हुईं।

panels.shot: low-angle wide focusing on Varaha relief
panels.action: The great Varaha relief fills the frame, the boar-lord lifting the small, graceful earth-goddess, with tiny human figures of sages and attendants carved below, Chandragupta II a small figure at the base.

dialogue.text: Scholars would later read this as royal theology: just as Varaha raises the world from chaos, so the king restores land and law, lifting his realm from the long unsettled age of foreign satraps.

i18n.hi: विद्वान बाद में इसे राजधर्म की मूर्ति पढ़ेंगे–जैसे वराह जगत को अराजकता से ऊपर उठाता है, वैसे ही राजा धरती और कानून को संभालकर अपने राज्य को परदेसी क्षत्रपों के लंबे उथल‑पुथल से बाहर खींचता है।

panels.shot: medium close-up of inscription being carved
panels.action: A stonecutter carefully chisels an inscription into the rock near a cave at Udayagiri, while Chandragupta II stands nearby, the carved letters crisp and elegant in the shadowed light.

dialogue.text: In a nearby cave, an inscription dated to Gupta Era eighty-two names Chandragupta the Second, fixing his presence here more firmly than any court poem or later legend of nine shining gems.

i18n.hi: पास की गुफा के अभिलेख में, गुप्त संवत बहासी में, चंद्रगुप्त द्वितीय का नाम खुदा है, जो उसे यहां किसी दरबारी कविता या नौ रत्नों की दंतकथा से भी पक्का बैठा देता है।

panels.shot: thoughtful medium close-up of Chandragupta before relief
panels.action: Chandragupta II, cloaked and unarmored, stands alone before the finished Varaha relief at dusk, his face lit by the last light as he looks up in contemplative silence.

dialogue.speaker: chandragupta_ii_vikramaditya
dialogue.text: Let power pass, but let order endure.

i18n.hi: शक्ति बदले तो बदले, पर व्यवस्था टिकी रहे।

panels.shot: timeless wide shot blending coins, relief, and coastline
panels.action: A symbolic composition shows the Varaha relief, a scatter of Gupta silver coins, and a distant western shoreline with ships, all layered like memories against a faded parchment sky.

dialogue.text: By ending Shaka rule, seizing the western ports, and binding god and kingship in stone at Udayagiri, Chandragupta Vikramaditya left a legacy where the truest monument was the ordered freedom of his people.

i18n.hi: शकों का राज मिटाकर, पश्चिमी बंदरगाह समेटकर और उदयगिरि पर देव‑राजा को पत्थर में बांधकर, चंद्रगुप्त विक्रमादित्य ने ऐसी विरासत छोड़ी जिसमें उसका असली स्मारक था उसके लोगों की सजी‑धजी आज़ादी।

title_i18n.en: Chandragupta II and the Fall of the Shakas
title_i18n.hi: चंद्रगुप्त द्वितीय और शकाओं का अंत

subtitle_i18n.en: How Vikramaditya Broke the Western Shaka Power and Raised the Boar of Udayagiri
subtitle_i18n.hi: कैसे विक्रमादित्य ने पश्चिमी शक शक्ति तोड़ी और उदयगिरि का वराह उठाया
